name: Nautilus
description: Identify the Nautilus by its rounded octagonal bezel with two hinge-like 'ears' on the left and right, the horizontally embossed dial grooves, and the integrated steel bracelet flowing straight out of the case. It may come in variants like: blue dial, green dial, white-gold, and rose-gold.

name: Aquanaut
description: Identify the Aquanaut by its rounded octagonal bezel over a cushion-shaped case, the embossed checkerboard 'grid' pattern on the dial, and the matching tropical rubber strap. It may come in variants like: black dial, blue dial, green dial, and the diamond-set Luce.

name: Cubitus
description: Identify the Cubitus by its large squared-off, rounded-square case, the horizontally grooved dial borrowed from the Nautilus, and the integrated bracelet. It may come in variants like: olive-green dial and blue-grey dial.

name: Calatrava
description: Identify the Calatrava by its purist round dress-watch case, an exceptionally clean dial with slim baton or dauphine hands, and a leather strap. It may come in variants like: white dial, silver dial, and the Clous de Paris (hobnail) bezel version.

name: Golden Ellipse
description: Identify the Golden Ellipse by its unmistakable elongated oval case, proportioned to the golden ratio, with a bare dial and only slim baton hands and markers. It may come in variants like: blue-gold sunburst dial and ebony-black dial.

name: Gondolo
description: Identify the Gondolo by its vertical Art Deco rectangular or tonneau case, frequently paired with Breguet numerals and a railway minute track. It may come in variants like: white dial, silver dial, and rose-gold.

name: Twenty~4
description: Identify the Twenty~4 by its horizontal 'cuff' rectangular case flanked by two columns of diamonds, designed as a ladies' watch. It may come in variants like: the quartz diamond-set manchette and the round automatic in steel.

name: World Time
description: Identify the World Time by its two concentric rotating rings — a ring of 24 world-city names around a 24-hour day/night ring — surrounding a guilloché or enamel centre, read with just an hour and minute hand. It may come in variants like: blue dial and the cloisonné enamel map centre.

name: Perpetual Calendar
description: Identify the Perpetual Calendar by its round case and cluster of sub-dials and apertures showing day, month, date, and moon phase, with the moon phase typically at 6 o'clock. It may come in variants like: white dial, cream dial, and versions with a leap-year indicator.
